# Rosalind Problem: Open Reading Frames (ORF)

## Problem Description
Given a DNA string, find all open reading frames (ORFs) that could potentially encode proteins. An ORF is a sequence that starts with a start codon (ATG) and ends with a stop codon (TAA, TAG, or TGA).

## Solution in Lightning Web Component

```javascript
// orf.js
import { LightningElement } from 'lwc';

export default class Orf extends LightningElement {
    dnaSequence = '';
    orfs = [];
    
    handleInputChange(event) {
        this.dnaSequence = event.target.value.toUpperCase();
        this.orfs = this.findOrfs(this.dnaSequence);
    }
    
    findOrfs(dna) {
        const orfs = [];
        const codons = this.getCodons(dna);
        
        // Find all start codons (ATG)
        const startPositions = [];
        for (let i = 0; i < codons.length; i++) {
            if (codons[i] === 'ATG') {
                startPositions.push(i);
            }
        }
        
        // For each start codon, find the first stop codon
        for (let startPos of startPositions) {
            for (let i = startPos; i < codons.length; i++) {
                const codon = codons[i];
                if (codon === 'TAA' || codon === 'TAG' || codon === 'TGA') {
                    // Found a stop codon, extract the ORF
                    const orfLength = (i - startPos + 1) * 3;
                    const orfSequence = dna.substring(startPos * 3, startPos * 3 + orfLength);
                    orfs.push(orfSequence);
                    break;
                }
            }
        }
        
        return orfs;
    }
    
    getCodons(dna) {
        const codons = [];
        for (let i = 0; i < dna.length; i += 3) {
            if (i + 3 <= dna.length) {
                codons.push(dna.substring(i, i + 3));
            }
        }
        return codons;
    }
    
    render() {
        return html`
            <div class="container">
                <h2>Open Reading Frame Finder</h2>
                <div class="input-section">
                    <label for="dnaSequence">DNA Sequence:</label>
                    <textarea 
                        id="dnaSequence" 
                        value={dnaSequence}
                        onchange={handleInputChange}
                        placeholder="Enter DNA sequence (e.g.
